verses.1: A Prayer of the afflicted when he is weak and pours out his complaint before the LORD. Hear my prayer, LORD! And let my cry for help come to You.
verses.2: Do not hide Your face from me on the day of my distress; Incline Your ear to me; On the day when I call answer me quickly.
verses.3: For my days have ended in smoke, And my bones have been scorched like a hearth.
verses.4: My heart has been struck like grass and has withered, Indeed, I forget to eat my bread.
verses.5: Because of the loudness of my groaning My bones cling to my flesh.
verses.6: I resemble a pelican of the wilderness; I have become like an owl of the ruins.
verses.7: I lie awake, I have become like a solitary bird on a housetop.
verses.8: My enemies have taunted me all day long; Those who deride me have used my name as a curse.
verses.9: For I have eaten ashes like bread, And mixed my drink with weeping
verses.10: Because of Your indignation and Your wrath; For You have lifted me up and thrown me away.
verses.11: My days are like a lengthened shadow, And I wither away like grass.
verses.12: But You, LORD, remain forever, And Your name remains to all generations.
verses.13: You will arise and have compassion on Zion; For it is time to be gracious to her, For the appointed time has come.
verses.14: Surely Your servants take pleasure in her stones, And feel pity for her dust.
verses.15: So the nations will fear the name of the LORD, And all the kings of the earth, Your glory.
verses.16: For the LORD has built up Zion; He has appeared in His glory.
verses.17: He has turned His attention to the prayer of the destitute And has not despised their prayer.
verses.18: This will be written for the generation to come, That a people yet to be created may praise the LORD:
verses.19: For He looked down from His holy height; From heaven the LORD looked upon the earth,
verses.20: To hear the groaning of the prisoner, To set free those who were doomed to death,
verses.21: So that people may tell of the name of the LORD in Zion, And His praise in Jerusalem,
verses.22: When the peoples are gathered together, And the kingdoms, to serve the LORD.
verses.23: He has broken my strength in the way; He has shortened my days.
verses.24: I say, “My God, do not take me away in the middle of my days, Your years are throughout all generations.
verses.25: “In time of old You founded the earth, And the heavens are the work of Your hands.
verses.26: “Even they will perish, but You endure; All of them will wear out like a garment; Like clothing You will change them and they will pass away.
verses.27: “But You are the same, And Your years will not come to an end.
verses.28: “The children of Your servants will continue, And their descendants will be established before You.”
